title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

description: Ah, Focaccia Genovese! This Ligurian masterpiece is a testament to simple ingredients creating extraordinary flavor. Its dimpled surface, glistening with olive oil and fragrant rosemary, promises a perfectly chewy interior and a delightful crispy crust. A true taste of Italy, perfect for sharing or savoring all by yourself.

ingredients.notes: plus coarse sea salt for sprinkling
plus more for greasing and drizzling

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm water, active dry yeast, and sugar (if using). Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. This indicates the yeast is active and ready.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour and fine sea salt. Pour in the activated yeast mixture and 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, cohesive dough forms.

instructions.title: Knead and First Rise
instructions.text: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Transfer the dough to a well-oiled bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Transfer and Dimple
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough into the pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. Create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough with your fingers.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Season
instructions.text: Drizzle the dough generously with more extra virgin olive oil, ensuring it pools slightly in the dimples. Sprinkle evenly with the chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425F (220C) during the last 15 minutes of the second rise. Bake the focaccia for 20-30 minutes, or until the top is beautifully golden brown and the crust is crispy. The aroma will fill your kitchen!

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven. If desired, you can brush with a little more olive oil for extra sheen and flavor. Let it cool in the pan for a few minutes before transferring it to a wire rack to cool slightly further. Slice into squares or strips and serve warm.

tips: For the crispiest bottom crust, preheat a baking stone or an inverted baking sheet in the oven before placing your focaccia pan on it.
Do not skimp on the olive oil! It is crucial for the flavor, texture, and the signature moistness of a true Genovese focaccia.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, store at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or freeze slices for longer storage.
article:
The irresistible aroma of freshly baked bread, kissed with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil, is a culinary dream for many. Among the myriad of Italian baking traditions, one stands out for its elegant simplicity and profound flavor: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. This flatbread, hailing from the Ligurian capital of Genoa, is more than just a side dish; it's a testament to the power of a few high-quality ingredients transformed into something truly sublime. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Italian bakery experience in your own kitchen, this guide will walk you through every step to master this beloved classic.

## What is Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil?
Focaccia Genovese is not just any focaccia; it's a specific, revered style originating from Genoa, Italy. Unlike some thicker, fluffier focaccias found elsewhere, the Genovese version is characterized by its relatively thin profile, incredibly soft and airy interior, and a distinctly crisp, golden crust. What truly sets it apart is the generous use of high-quality extra virgin olive oil, not just in the dough, but also drizzled lavishly on top before baking. This olive oil creates those signature dimples and a salty, slightly slick surface that is utterly addictive. The addition of fresh rosemary imbues the bread with an earthy, piney fragrance that perfectly complements the olive oil's peppery notes. The result is a bread that manages to be both rustic and refined, a staple in Ligurian households and a beloved treat worldwide. Making authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a rewarding culinary journey that connects you directly to the heart of Italian baking.

## Ingredients You Need
Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil requires a commitment to quality ingredients. Each component plays a vital role in achieving that authentic texture and flavor.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (Type 00 or All-Purpose with high protein):** Approximately 500g. A higher protein content (12-14%) is ideal for developing strong gluten, which gives focaccia its characteristic chewiness and open crumb structure.
*   **Lukewarm Water:** Approximately 350-400ml. The exact amount can vary based on flour type and humidity, so add gradually. It should be around 105-115°F (40-46°C) to activate the yeast.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (about 2 ¼ teaspoons). This is the leavening agent that makes the focaccia rise and gives it its airy texture.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g (about 1 ¾ teaspoons) for the dough, plus extra for sprinkling on top. Salt is crucial for flavor and also helps control yeast activity.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** 50-70ml for the dough, plus a generous amount (at least 50-70ml) for drizzling on top and for the pan. Use the best quality you can find, as its flavor truly shines through.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, finely chopped, plus a few small sprigs for garnish. Fresh is non-negotiable for that vibrant, aromatic punch.

## Step-by-Step Instructions
Patience and attention to detail are key to mastering Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. Follow these steps for a truly authentic result.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the lukewarm water with the active dry yeast and a pinch of sugar (optional, but helps activate yeast). Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and 10g of fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in 50ml of extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead the Dough:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 10-15 minutes until the dough is smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. If using a stand mixer, knead with a dough hook for 7-10 minutes on medium speed. The dough should pass the "windowpane test" (stretch a small piece until it's thin enough to see light through without tearing).
4.  **First Proof:** Lightly oil a clean bowl. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5-2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Generously coat a large baking sheet (approximately 13x18 inches or 33x46 cm) with extra virgin olive oil. Don't be shy; this oil is essential for the crispy bottom crust.
6.  **Shape the Focaccia:** Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared baking sheet. Using your oiled fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan. It might resist at first, so let it rest for 10-15 minutes if needed, then continue stretching. Aim for an even thickness.
7.  **Second Proof & Dimpling:** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel. Let it rise again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes. Once slightly risen, create the signature dimples by pressing your oiled fingertips firmly into the dough, almost to the bottom of the pan.
8.  **Season and Bake:** Drizzle another generous amount of extra virgin olive oil over the dimpled dough. Sprinkle evenly with chopped fresh rosemary and a good amount of coarse sea salt.
9.  **Preheat Oven:** Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
10. **Bake:** Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is golden brown on top and the bottom is crisp and golden.
11. **Cool and Serve:** Remove from the oven and transfer the focaccia to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm or at room temperature.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
Achieving that authentic taste and texture of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil involves a few crucial nuances.

*   **High Hydration is Key:** Focaccia dough is typically very wet. Don't be afraid if it feels sticky during kneading; resist the urge to add too much extra flour. The high water content contributes to the airy, open crumb. Oiling your hands helps manage stickiness.
*   **Quality Olive Oil Matters:** This is not the time to skimp on olive oil. A high-quality extra virgin olive oil will impart a superior flavor and aroma that is central to the Genovese style.
*   **Generous Oiling:** Both the pan and the top of the focaccia need a liberal amount of olive oil. This prevents sticking, promotes a beautiful golden crust, and creates those delightful crispy edges.
*   **Proper Dimpling:** Don't be shy when dimpling. Press your fingertips down firmly, almost to the bottom of the pan. These depressions are where the olive oil pools, creating pockets of intense flavor and a characteristic texture.
*   **Don't Overbake:** Keep an eye on the color. You want a beautiful golden-brown crust, but overbaking can dry out the interior. The bottom should also be golden and crisp.
*   **Listen to Your Dough:** The proofing times are guidelines. Pay attention to the dough itself – it should be visibly risen and puffy, not just following a clock. Environmental factors like room temperature can affect rising times.

## Serving Suggestions
Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways.

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve it simply with a bowl of good quality extra virgin olive oil for dipping, perhaps with a sprinkle of balsamic vinegar. It's also fantastic alongside olives, cured meats, and cheeses as part of an antipasto platter.
*   **With Soups and Salads:** Its hearty yet light texture makes it an excellent accompaniment to a robust minestrone, a creamy tomato soup, or a fresh, vibrant caprese salad.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally to create delicious sandwiches. Fill with prosciutto and provolone, grilled vegetables and pesto, or a simple combination of fresh mozzarella and sun-dried tomatoes.
*   **Alongside Main Courses:** It pairs wonderfully with grilled fish, roasted chicken, or any Mediterranean-inspired main dish.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch:** Enjoy a slice with a fried egg, or simply with a cup of coffee. The savory notes are surprisingly delightful in the morning.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is perfect as is, there's always room for creative twists once you've mastered the basic recipe.

*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, feel free to experiment with other fresh herbs. Thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs can add different aromatic profiles.
*   **Toppings:** Add sliced cherry tomatoes, black olives (like Taggiasca olives, common in Liguria), thinly sliced onions, or even a sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese during the last few minutes of baking.
*   **Garlic Infusion:** Mince a clove or two of garlic and mix it with the olive oil before drizzling it over the focaccia for an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Spicy Kick:** A pinch of red pepper flakes sprinkled on top with the rosemary can add a subtle warmth.
*   **Flour Blends:** For a slightly nuttier flavor and denser texture, you can substitute a small percentage (up to 25%) of the bread flour with whole wheat flour. However, for true Genovese authenticity, stick with white bread flour.
*   **Vegan Options:** The base recipe is inherently vegan, using only plant-based ingredients.

Mastering Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a truly rewarding experience. From the simple act of mixing the dough to the moment you pull that fragrant, golden-brown loaf from the oven, you're engaging in a culinary tradition that has brought joy to generations. Its humble ingredients belie a depth of flavor and texture that makes it an unforgettable bread. So gather your ingredients, clear your counter, and prepare to embark on a delicious journey to the heart of Italian baking. Your kitchen, and your taste buds, will thank you.
